title: Goroutines, Channels, Select, Ownership, and Pipelines · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A report pipeline must process bounded work concurrently, preserve result ownership, close channels correctly, and terminate every goroutine. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a bounded concurrent report pipeline while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Start goroutines only with named ownership, inputs, outputs, stop conditions, panic boundary, and completion evidence.
Use unbuffered, buffered, and directional channels while assigning close authority to the sender that knows no values remain.
Distinguish open, closed, empty, nil, and buffered channel states and interpret two-result receives and range termination.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Goroutine Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Pipeline 6 starts 8 workers, has a two-slot buffer, one early consumer exit, two simultaneously ready select cases, and one unclosed output. Trace ownership, blocking, close authority, result order, and every goroutine's stop path.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Goroutine Lifecycle.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for start goroutines only with named ownership, inputs, outputs, stop conditions, panic boundary, and completion evidence. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Goroutine Lifecycle, keep Goroutine Lifecycle connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
